title: Rubaiyat: The Quatrains
description: Omar Khayyam's quatrains on wine, mortality, and the unknowability of the cosmos.
introduction: Omar Khayyam was a mathematician, astronomer, and philosopher whose short quatrains (rubaiyat) express a radical skepticism about the afterlife and a fierce insistence on living fully in the present. His hedonism is philosophical, not trivial — the wine he celebrates is both literal and a metaphor for intoxication with the mystery of existence. Edward FitzGerald's 1859 English translation made the Rubaiyat one of the most quoted poems in the English language.

translation.original_text: اوقات خوش آن بود که با دوست به سر شد / باقی همه بی‌حاصلی و بی‌خبری بود
translation.text: The happy times were those spent with a friend — the rest was all fruitlessness and ignorance.

translation.original_text: این قافلهٔ عمر عجب می‌گذرد / دریاب دمی که با طرب می‌گذرد
translation.text: This caravan of life passes wondrous quickly — seize the moment that passes with joy.
